SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.chemistry.scale

Class BrineMixingScaleEvaluator

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.chemistry.scale.BrineMixingScaleEvaluator
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class BrineMixingScaleEvaluator extends Object implements Serializable
Mixing-induced scale evaluation for two incompatible brines (for example seawater injection breaking through into formation water).

Sulphate scales (BaSO4, SrSO4, CaSO4) are the classic incompatibility problem: sulphate-rich seawater mixed with a barium/strontium-rich formation water precipitates sulphate scale even though neither brine is scaling on its own. The worst supersaturation frequently occurs at an intermediate mixing ratio, not at either end member.

This evaluator linearly blends the two brine ion analyses across a set of mixing fractions (fraction of brine A in the mixture), evaluates an activity-corrected ElectrolyteScaleCalculator at each blend, and reports the saturation index of each mineral versus mixing fraction plus the worst-case fraction and mineral. Common-ion effects are captured because the shared ions (SO4, Ba, Sr, Ca) are mixed on a mass-concentration basis before the saturation calculation.

Standards informational: NACE TM0374, Oddo-Tomson, NORSOK M-001.

Version:
1.0
Author:
ESOL
See Also:
  • ElectrolyteScaleCalculator

  • Constructor Details

    • BrineMixingScaleEvaluator

      public BrineMixingScaleEvaluator(BrineMixingScaleEvaluator.BrineComposition brineA, BrineMixingScaleEvaluator.BrineComposition brineB)
      Constructs an evaluator for two brines.
      Parameters:
      brineA - the first brine end member (for example formation water); must not be null
      brineB - the second brine end member (for example injection seawater); must not be null

  • Method Details

    • setConditions

      public BrineMixingScaleEvaluator setConditions(double temperatureC, double pressureBara)
      Sets the temperature and pressure at which the mixture is evaluated.
      Parameters:
      temperatureC - temperature in degrees Celsius
      pressureBara - pressure in bara
      Returns:
      this for chaining

    • setPH

      public BrineMixingScaleEvaluator setPH(double pH)
      Sets the pH of the mixed brine.
      Parameters:
      pH - pH of the aqueous phase
      Returns:
      this for chaining

    • setSteps

      public BrineMixingScaleEvaluator setSteps(int steps)
      Sets the number of mixing fractions evaluated between the two end members (minimum 2).
      Parameters:
      steps - number of mixing points
      Returns:
      this for chaining

    • evaluate

      public BrineMixingScaleEvaluator evaluate()
      Evaluates the mineral saturation across the mixing range and records the worst-case point.
      Returns:
      this for chaining

    • blend

      private double blend(double a, double b, double fA, double fB)
      Blends two concentrations by mixing fractions.
      Parameters:
      a - concentration in brine A
      b - concentration in brine B
      fA - fraction of brine A
      fB - fraction of brine B
      Returns:
      the blended concentration

    • updateWorst

      private void updateWorst(double fractionA, String mineral, double si)
      Updates the worst-case record if the supplied saturation index is the highest seen so far.
      Parameters:
      fractionA - the mixing fraction of brine A at this point
      mineral - the mineral name
      si - the saturation index

    • getPoints

      public List<BrineMixingScaleEvaluator.MixPoint> getPoints()
      Gets the list of evaluated mixing points.
      Returns:
      the mixing-fraction result points

    • getWorstFractionA

      public double getWorstFractionA()
      Gets the mixing fraction of brine A at which the worst-case supersaturation occurs.
      Returns:
      worst-case fraction of brine A (0 to 1)

    • getWorstMineral

      public String getWorstMineral()
      Gets the mineral with the highest saturation index across the mixing range.
      Returns:
      the worst-case mineral name

    • getWorstSI

      public double getWorstSI()
      Gets the highest saturation index across the mixing range.
      Returns:
      the worst-case saturation index

    • ensureEvaluated

      private void ensureEvaluated()
      Ensures evaluate() has been called before returning a result.

    • toJson

      Serialises the mixing sweep and worst-case summary to a JSON string.
